title: How much of Platinum was re dubbed
display_title: How much of Platinum was re dubbed
wikitext:
[]: How much of Platinum was re-dubbed???             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Wed Sep 20, 2006 6:35 pm I got the Platinum thinpack recently, which made me giddy as a schoolgirl, and because I'm a total dork I had to watch everything in both languages. I was left with some questions after watching the dub for the whole series. I watched the Director's Cut episodes first, and it was obvious that large portions of them had been re-dubbed to accomodate the new material. Surprisingly though, it seemed more like the NPC episodes in their entirety had been redubbed, as opposed to just the additional scenes. This was espectially striking in Ep. 21, because I've watched the dub version of that episode many times and I could tell that even though Fuyutsuki was played by the same VA, his delivery was very different in certain scenes. Still, I wasn't too surprised that there was redubbing for the NPC eps. What started to bother me was that, when I watched the series over again from the beginning, it sounded like certain lines had been selectively redubbed. It didn't sound like any episodes had been redubbed in their entirely, but either my memory is failing or some specific lines/conversations were redone, in terms of both acting and translation. One example is in ep. 5 when Ritsuko is explaining to Shinji how Gendoh burned his hands, and in Platinum Shinji says "Bullshit. My father did that?" I'm pretty sure he didn't say bullshit in the original dub, because I think I would have remembered that. The only way for me to figure this out for sure would be to bust out my old dub VHS tapes and compare and contrast, but that seems like a lot of work for no real benefit; someone here probably already knows the answer to this question. Sorry if this has been asked before, I looked around but didn't find anything on this topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Wed Sep 20, 2006 6:46 pm In the part with the "Bullshit. My father did that?", was Shinji wearing a hat? If so I know what you're getting at and I believe it may have been re-dubbed.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Sep 20, 2006 10:46 pm The NPC eps were redubbed a couple years back for the Director's Cut releases. The rest of the eps for the renewal only had selected lines re-dubbed. For example, adding in "Bullshit" to that line, or in another case, in episode 2 Changing a Misato line so she was concerned about Contamination, rather than Brain Damage.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 21, 2006 6:26 am Adding "Bullshit" makes no sense, considering there is no Japanese basis for it (such as "Uso!", or whatever).

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 21, 2006 8:34 am Reichu wrote: Adding "Bullshit" makes no sense, considering there is no Japanese basis for it (such as "Uso!", or whatever).

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Thu Sep 21, 2006 10:31 am Even I could tell something was up with the dubbing. And I only watched the first eight episodes in 2000. Cool to have it confirmed =D

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Thu Sep 21, 2006 12:26 pm In episode 3 (I believe), when Shinji is getting ready to go out to face Shamshel, he usually says "Yeah..." twice in a very reluctant tone before Misato issues the command to launch Eva. They took that out in Platinum. Also in episode 22, they added Ritsuko calling Asuka a bitch for rushing out on her own. I thought that was highly inappropriate. It adds fuel to the fire of "they turned Ritsuko into evil witch woman". (Edit: How could I forget this? They also removed Pen-Pen's humming from the hot springs scene in 10. BLASPHEMOUS.)

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Thu Sep 21, 2006 1:27 pm Good, I'm not crazy. The first change I noticed was to Misato's line about contamination in ep. 2, I thought I was hearing things. The changes they made seem kind of arbitrary though, I wonder if they really gave it a lot of thought or it was just kind of like, "Hey, Allison, as long as you're in the studio why don't you just say this..." Ritsuko's use of "bitch" stood out to me too, but it really didn't bother me. Some people make a big deal about the amount of profanity added to the Eva dub in general, but I don't really think it's a fair comparison since profanity is used so much more lightly in English in general. I know it's controversial though.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Sep 21, 2006 1:44 pm My first thought on "bullshit" was that someone in the office decided that the first episodes of the series weren't 'hardcore' enough compared with the later ones, so a little profanity needed sprinkling in order to inspire n00b rationalization of "he said 'bullshit'" = "not a kids' show". Of course, the new word handed over to ADV's Bitch Ritsuko (hereon referred to as BR in order to distinguish her from the real one) seems to strengthen the thought that someone at ADV has this idea in their head that there will be the old, poopy GAINAX version of the series, and the incredible, amazing, super hardcore (now with 3% more swearing) ADV version. I dunno. I didn't even realize they had changed anything until someone mentioned it.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Sep 21, 2006 2:12 pm The only line I know of that they changed that was not arbitrary was Misato's concern over contamination. They also redid all the previews.

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Thu Sep 21, 2006 3:22 pm Eh, I don't really have strong feelings one way or the other about the inclusion of Shinji's "bullshit" line, but I think sometimes it has to be taken into account that a swearword may be added not specifically because it's "dirty", but because it has a certain connotation that it would be otherwise difficult to get across in only one word. For example, bullshit in that context says to me "You've got to be kidding, it doesn't seem like this is the kind of thing you would make up, and yet this is completely inconsistent with everything I thought I knew about the topic (my father), you had better elaborate here." Would a more neutral word like "really?" really have gotten that whole idea across as effectively? Granted, if they could have thought of a cleaner word to use to get the same idea across that would have been preferable, but I honestly can't think of one. Obviously my point in posting this question was not to defend the dubbing changes, just asking about them, but I do find it to be an interesting topic nontheless...I don't agree with everything the ADR writers did for Eva, but I do think it's a much more complex process than many fans are willing to admit. Sometimes the most "accurate" translation in terms of word choice may not get the same idea across in a different language at all, it really requires a case-by-case analysis, which is why it's so volatile. Everyone's going to disagree with SOME choices they make.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Sep 21, 2006 4:35 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: My first thought on "bullshit" was that someone in the office decided that the first episodes of the series weren't 'hardcore' enough compared with the later ones, so a little profanity needed sprinkling in order to inspire n00b rationalization of "he said 'bullshit'" = "not a kids' show". Of course, the new word handed over to ADV's Bitch Ritsuko (hereon referred to as BR in order to distinguish her from the real one) seems to strengthen the thought that someone at ADV has this idea in their head that there will be the old, poopy GAINAX version of the series, and the incredible, amazing, super hardcore (now with 3% more swearing) ADV version. I dunno. I didn't even realize they had changed anything until someone mentioned it.

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Thu Sep 21, 2006 6:14 pm If memory serves it bothered me in GITS because it was profanity used strictly as expletives (like Kusanagi yelling "Shit!"), it wasn't used in any way where I could see it and think "Okay, I can see why they might have felt the need to use this specific word." It was just kinda there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 21, 2006 6:18 pm zeaven wrote: In episode 3 (I believe), when Shinji is getting ready to go out to face Shamshel, he usually says "Yeah..." twice in a very reluctant tone before Misato issues the command to launch Eva. They took that out in Platinum.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 21, 2006 9:45 pm Mmkay, the biggest problem with "Bullshit" is that there is NO Japanese dialogue for it to dub over. In Japanese, Shinji just says (translated), "My father did that?" That's it. Nothing else. Whether dubbing is a deadly sin in and of itself aside, one of the deadly sins of dubbing is putting dialogue (or whatever) where there is none. "ADV, time to bend over and say hello to Mr. Broom."

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Sep 21, 2006 9:53 pm Some needs to beat ADV down. Badly. Let's put our money together and try to buy the rights...

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Thu Sep 21, 2006 11:27 pm Reichu wrote: Mmkay, the biggest problem with "Bullshit" is that there is NO Japanese dialogue for it to dub over. In Japanese, Shinji just says (translated), "My father did that?" That's it. Nothing else.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 22, 2006 8:43 am CorporalChaos wrote: Some needs to beat ADV down. Badly. Let's put our money together and try to buy the rights...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 22, 2006 12:52 pm Karen_Leslie wrote: I get what you're saying, but at the same time I think that line of argument is somewhat weakened by the fact that Japanese and English are so different, even if the sentiment of the original corresponded more to the English "Bull!", it's highly unlikely that an equivalent Japanese word would be there; it would be in large part taken from the context and the delivery.

[]:             Quote Post by Karen_Leslie » Fri Sep 22, 2006 1:09 pm With all due respect, I think you may be missing my point. Once you've acknowledged the fact that a 'spiritually' accurate translation may actually have to utilize words that have very little in common with the original Japanese dialogue, you're on a very slippery slope where everything is highly subjective. For you, you don't think an English exclamation/curse should ever be put in unless there was a comprable Japanese word like "chikusho!" in there, but many would disagree. A Japanese protagonist might say something with a lot of emotion behind it without there being a direct exclamation in the original dialogue, and an English translator striving for accuracy might include an English exclamation, because the emotion in the delivery will not get through if the (typically indirect and context-sensitive) Japanese line were translated more or less directly. If you're going to say for translation purposes that exclamation=exclamation, then it should follow that adjective=adjective, noun=noun etc., and we all know that's not really the case. This is why many people argue that translation is closer to an artform than anything else. In case this wasn't clear, I think in the case that sparked this conversation Shinji's line was, if not technically empirically wrong, unnessesarily exaggerated. I just got on this topic because responses on this forum concerning translation seem to often be representative of ideas fans like to have about the subject for the ease of launching their own opinionated diatribes, and with little to no knowledge of how translation actually works. If you have translation experience in some areas, whatever methods you use probably work well for you, but translators who work in different media might have many differing opinions. It's a very complex subject where "well they didn't say X in Japanese but they said it English" is always, always problematic. Freakin' "Hai" doesn't mean "Yes" all the time for crying out loud. You see what I'm saying?

I was left with some questions after watching the dub for the whole series. I watched the Director's Cut episodes first, and it was obvious that large portions of them had been re

Article: How much of Platinum was re dubbed
 really have strong feelings one way or the other about the inclusion of Shinji's "bullshit" line, but I think sometimes it has to be taken into account that a swearword may be added not specifically because it's "dirty", but because it has a certain connotation that it would be otherwise difficult to get across in only one word. For example, bullshit in that context says to me "You've got to be kidding, it doesn't seem like this is the kind of thing you would make up, and yet this is completely inconsistent with everything I thought I knew about the topic (my father), you had better elaborate here." Would a more neutral word like "really?" really have gotten that whole idea across as effectively? Granted, if they could have thought of a cleaner word to use to get the same idea across that would have been preferable, but I honestly can't think of one. Obviously my point in posting this question was not to defend the dubbing changes, just asking about them, but I do find it to be an interesting topic nontheless...I don't agree with everything the ADR writers did for Eva, but I do think it's a much more complex process than many fans are willing to admit. Sometimes the most "accurate" translation in terms of word choice may not get the same idea across in a different language at all, it really requires a case-by-case analysis, which is why it's so volatile. Everyone's going to disagree with SOME choices they make.
